question_text: How would the gravitational force between two objects change if the distance between them doubled?

options.label: C
options.text: It would reduce to one quarter

correct_answer: It would reduce to one quarter
explanation: Gravitational force follows an inverse square law. This means if you double the distance between two objects, the gravitational force decreases by a factor of 2² = 4. So the force becomes one quarter (¼) of its original strength.
